chunk_summary: This section discusses shared care planning, referrals, and linkage to Indian nutrition programs for addressing pediatric nutrition issues through inter-professional care and follow-up monitoring.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of inter-professional care in managing pediatric nutrition problems.
Learn how to create and implement a shared care plan for nutrition-related issues.
Identify appropriate referral triggers for specialized care in pediatric nutrition.
Familiarize with Indian nutrition programs like ICDS, RBSK, POSHAN Abhiyaan, and MAA.
key_facts: A shared care plan should include diagnosis, baseline measures (e.g., weight/height trajectory, BMI percentile, MUAC, BP percentile), 1-2 behavioral goals, a monitoring plan, and escalation triggers.
Anganwadi/ICDS supports growth monitoring and supplementary nutrition for children under 6 years.
RBSK enables screening and referral for deficiencies and developmental concerns.
POSHAN Abhiyaan focuses on program convergence to improve nutrition outcomes.
MAA (Mother’s Absolute Affection) strengthens breastfeeding practices and lactation support.
A single shared metric (e.g., milk volume/day or sugary drinks/week) ensures consistent messaging across clinicians.
reference_quote: Anganwadi/ICDS supports under-6 growth monitoring and supplementary nutrition; RBSK enables screening and referral for deficiencies and developmental concerns; POSHAN supports convergence; and MAA strengthens breastfeeding practices.
document_key_topics: Shared care planning in pediatric nutrition
